matches.match_rationale: The core pain is attendance data arriving in incompatible formats (biometric exports, manual timesheets, spreadsheets) from multiple campuses. Data Transformation directly addresses this by normalizing heterogeneous semi-structured inputs into a unified schema suitable for payroll computation. This is precisely the 'reshape, clean, and map to a target schema' use case the capability is designed for.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An AI-assisted ETL layer that ingests attendance files from each campus in whatever format they arrive, maps fields to a canonical schema, flags missing or malformed records, and outputs a clean consolidated dataset ready for gross pay calculation — reducing manual consolidation from hours to minutes per payroll cycle.

matches.match_rationale: Some campuses rely on paper timesheets or scanned/photographed attendance sheets submitted as PDFs or images. OCR and form-field extraction can digitize these documents into structured records, eliminating the manual transcription step that introduces errors and delays gross pay computation.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A document ingestion pipeline that accepts scanned timesheets or exported PDFs, extracts employee IDs, hours, and dates via OCR, and feeds the structured output directly into the consolidation workflow — removing the need for staff to manually re-enter handwritten or printed time data.

matches.match_rationale: After consolidation, attendance records frequently contain outliers — duplicate entries from biometric retries, implausible hours, or missing clock-outs — that require manual review before payroll runs. Anomaly detection can automatically flag these deviations, shifting staff from hunting for errors to reviewing a short exception list.
matches.opportunity_sketch: A post-consolidation anomaly layer that compares each employee's consolidated hours against their historical patterns and campus norms, surfaces a prioritized exception report for payroll staff to resolve before the run, cutting the manual audit step significantly.

matches.match_rationale: The full payroll-prep workflow — fetching files from each campus system, parsing formats, reconciling discrepancies, flagging exceptions, and loading into the payroll engine — is a multi-step process with branching logic (different formats per campus, retry on missing data). A task automation agent can orchestrate this end-to-end, replacing a manual sequence of spreadsheet manipulations and file transfers that currently spans multiple staff hours.
matches.opportunity_sketch: An autonomous payroll-prep agent that runs on a schedule, collects attendance exports from all campus systems via API or SFTP, normalizes and reconciles them, escalates unresolvable conflicts to a human, and deposits a verified consolidated file into the payroll system — turning a half-day manual task into a supervised 30-minute review.
